name: Nanakuli Beach Park
tagline: A wide sandy beach on the Waianae coast with some of the most fiery sunsets you'll see on the island.
bestTime: Late afternoon for golden light and sunsets; summer for calmer swimming
talkStory: Nanakuli means 'look at knee' or 'pretend to be deaf' in Hawaiian — local lore says it refers to residents who pretended not to hear travelers asking for food during a famine, out of shame at having none to share. The sunsets from this west-facing coast are the most vivid on Oahu because there is nothing but open ocean to the horizon. This is a tight-knit Native Hawaiian community beach; come with genuine aloha and respect and you will be welcomed warmly. Summer water is gentle; winter surf can be dangerous.
